subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall structure with introduction, method, results and conclusion
Numbered sections, labelled figures and tables placed near relevant text
Logical sequence of mathematical construction (mid-points â bisectors â Voronoi)
weaknesses: Occasional digressions into non-mathematical background reduced focus
Inconsistent formatting (repetitive captions, page breaks in mid-sentence)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report is coherent and follows a recognizable structure, unnecessary repetition, digressions into background material and minor formatting errors prevent it from reaching band 3. The work sits solidly at band 2 (adequate organization and mostly relevant content), meriting 2/4.

strengths: Use of conventional notation for mid-points, bisectors and distance formulas
Multiple representations including maps, Voronoi diagrams and summary tables
Explanations generally accompany calculations to aid understanding
weaknesses: Blurry or unlabeled diagrams and inconsistent unit presentation impaired clarity
Missing definitions (e.g., site H introduced without coordinates) and occasional notation lapses
Swapping between units (km vs grid units) and inconsistent rounding rules
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and representations are used effectively at times, but inconsistencies in notation, diagram quality and unit handling keep the work in band 2 (some appropriate language/representations). A score of 2/4 reflects solid but uneven mathematical communication.

strengths: Clear personal motivation linked to optometry career goals
Use of own home coordinates and choice of Voronoi method shows initiative
Integration of Google Maps and Desmos overlays demonstrates creativity
weaknesses: Mathematical approach follows standard procedures without original extensions
No exploration of alternative metrics or weighting to deepen personal contribution
Limited evidence of outstanding independent thinking beyond the chosen method
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration reflects a genuine personal approach and significant initiative, but stops short of outstanding originality or extended methods. Performance aligns with band 2, justifying 2/3.

strengths: Meaningful discussion of limitations (straight-line distance, zoning laws, rounding)
Reflection links back to understanding of Voronoi diagrams and site selection
Some suggestions for model improvements (network analysis, regression)
weaknesses: No quantitative sensitivity or alternative-metric analysis to deepen reflection
Critical analysis remains at a basic level without in-depth numerical exploration
Connections to enhanced understanding are asserted rather than demonstrated with further work
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and meaningful, but lacks depth and critical rigour to reach band 3. The consistent but limited analysis places it in band 2, meriting 2/3.

strengths: Correct application of mid-points, perpendicular bisectors and Euclidean distance
Relevant SL-level mathematics used appropriately in spatial planning context
Clear explanation of geometric principles behind the Voronoi construction
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies and unexplained identical distance results
Lack of sophisticated or alternative mathematical extensions (e.g., weighted diagrams)
Imprecise definitions (e.g., perpendicular bisector) and omitted intermediate steps in calculations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and mostly correct at course level, but imprecision, minor errors and absence of rigorous extension keep the work in band 2. A score of 2/6 reflects adequate but not fully developed use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting Voronoi diagrams to solve a personal location problem shows some independent choice of method, but the mathematics follows standard textbook procedures with limited original extension.  Evidence of initiative is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

The student demonstrates solid understanding of the geometric principles behind Voronoi diagrams and distance measures, explaining each step.  However, there is little extension into weighted or alternative diagrams, so understanding is good in parts rather than thorough with rigour.

Numerical work is mostly accurate; spot checks on the AB bisector and distance calculations reproduce the studentâs values.  Minor rounding inconsistencies (e.g., midpoint rounded while later used unrounded) and the unexplained identical distances of 18 units for V1 and V2 indicate âmostly correctâ accuracy.

Most of the text is relevant to locating a new optician, yet there is noticeable redundancy (multiple explanations of why opticians are needed, re-stating the unit conversion several times).  A few paragraphs digress into career aspirations without supporting the mathematics.  Overall conciseness is acceptable but not strong.

Key terms such as âperpendicular bisectorâ, âmid-pointâ and variables are defined, and notation in worked examples is mostly conventional.  There are minor lapsesârandom capitalisation ("Site"), missing absolute-value bars in distance formula reproductionâso language is âmostlyâ rather than âfullyâ appropriate.

Explanations usually accompany calculations, allowing the reader to reproduce results.  Inconsistenciesâsuch as swapping between units and kilometres, and a sudden unexplained âHâ siteâimpair full consistency, yet overall clarity is good.

Perpendicular bisectors, mid-points, linear equations and Euclidean distance are all standard SL topics and are applied correctly to a spatial-planning context.  The mathematics is relevant and at course level but not used in an especially efficient or sophisticated way.

The student employs maps, overlaid grids, Voronoi diagrams and summary tables, which together give multiple representations of the problem.  The diagrams are generally fit for purpose but some are blurry, axes lack scale labels and one vertex map is hard to read.  Hence representation is effective but not sophisticated.

The student reflects on limitations such as straight-line distance, zoning laws and rounding errors, showing meaningful engagement with the implications of results.  Reflection is present throughout but lacks depth in assessing how these factors might alter conclusions quantitatively.

Using Google Maps, Desmos overlays and self-generated data exhibits creativity, but there is little attempt to broaden the methodology (e.g., alternative distance metrics, weighting).  Initiative is therefore âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

The report follows a clear sequenceâintroduction, method, results, conclusion and limitations.  The mathematical construction (mid-points â bisectors â Voronoi â distance test) is developed step-by-step so the reader can follow the argument without major gaps.  Occasional digressions (e.g., long health-care motivation paragraphs) and some minor jumps between narrative and calculation reduce the overall flow, so the exploration is short of the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Some critical analysis is evident when the student discusses how network analysis or regression could improve the model; however, no alternative calculations are attempted.  Analysis goes beyond description but is not âdeepâ.

The reflections link back to understanding of Voronoi diagrams and how results influence site selection, showing a clear connection to learning.  Enhanced understanding is asserted rather than demonstrated with further mathematical work, keeping the strand in the âclear connectionâ band.

A contents page, numbered sections and labelled figures/tables give the work a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure.  Graphs and tables appear close to the text that references them.  Nevertheless, inconsistencies such as repetitive figure captions, page breaks in the middle of sentences and an unfinished sentence on p.16 keep the organization from being seamless.

The personal narrative links optometry career goals to the investigation, and the studentâs own home coordinate is used, giving a clear personal approach that drives the exploration.  The approach stops short of tailoring the mathematics beyond the basic model.

annotations.comment: The definition of a perpendicular bisector is imprecise: it bisects a segment at its midpoint at a right angle. Revise this wording to reinforce mathematical accuracy.
The midpoint calculation is correct, but consider adding notation such as $$x_m$$ and $$y_m$$ definitions within your text to support mathematical communication.
Rounding in Table 2 is inconsistent: the slope $$-\frac{707}{55}$$ is given as -12.9 (1 dp) rather than to 2 dp. Standardize to the stated 2 dp for consistency.
The diagram of perpendicular bisectors (Figure 2) is an effective representation, but the image is somewhat blurred. A higher resolution or vector graphic would enhance clarity.
Specifying â1 unit = 0.109 kmâ is essential; however, the source of this conversion is not cited. Add a reference or calculation to justify this factor.
Site H appears in the new diagram without prior introduction or coordinate listing. Ensure all sites are defined before use to maintain consistency.
When introducing $$d=\sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2}$$, include absolute-value notation or explain the choice of sign to avoid ambiguity in distance calculations.
Figure 5 clearly identifies Voronoi vertices, which aids understanding of potential locations. This visual strengthens your geometric analysis.
The verticesâ coordinates are neatly tabulated, but the table heading merges columns ambiguously. Adjust the heading span or labeling for clearer presentation.
The computation for V2 to site E correctly applies the distance formula, yet the intermediate arithmetic (e.g., squared differences) could be shown to strengthen mathematical rigor.
Table 4 summarises vertex distances effectively, but it omits the converted kilometer values. Including both units would enhance clarity and completeness.
Figure 6 introduces the new Voronoi diagram with V3 as a site, which shows initiative. Ensure that its caption accurately reflects all relevant changes to prevent confusion.

The title clearly states the investigationâs focus on locating an optician but could be more concise by removing redundancy (ânew Opticiansâ) or specifying context. Consider tightening the phrasing for clarity and precision.
The scoring table template is included but left blank. Ensure to input your achieved scores or remove unused table elements to maintain a polished presentation.
The table of contents is structured but contains blank page references for some sub-sections (e.g., âConstructing a Voronoi diagramâ). Complete all page numbers to improve navigability.
Several entries wrap inconsistently and some labels (e.g., âObtaining pointsââ) could be indented to reflect hierarchy more clearly. Refine formatting for improved coherence.
This background section includes useful context but spends excessive length on general trends in digital eye strain. Focus more tightly on mathematical rationale to enhance relevance and conciseness.
The inclusion of personal career aspirations and your own home coordinate demonstrates genuine engagement. This personal approach effectively motivates the mathematical exploration.
Your rationale for using Google Maps is clear, but the spelling of âguaranteeingâ is incorrect and should be corrected. Careful proofreading ensures professional presentation.
Figure 1 visually locates the sites but lacks a scale or legend, making spatial interpretation difficult. Include clearer axis labels or a scale bar for improved mathematical communication.
Coordinates are presented to two decimal places, but rounding is inconsistent (e.g., .75 vs whole number). Apply uniform rounding rules and state them in the caption for clarity.
The concluding sentence is cut off (âThis isâ). Review page breaks to avoid interrupted thoughts and ensure your argument flows cohesively.
Your conclusion reiterates practical benefits but lacks mathematical reflection on resultsâ sensitivity. Consider briefly discussing how varying parameters might shift the optimal site.
You list multiple qualitative limitations but do not quantitatively assess their impact. Including a brief sensitivity analysis or alternative metric would deepen your reflection.
The caption âFigure 1: Map of labelled sitesâ is informative, but consider positioning it directly below the figure and formatting it consistently with other captions.
